scope: ConstantPlanner constant-speed state continuity and controlled same-direction relative-motion ordering in scene-0383
hard_constraints: exactly one actor at every recorded timestamp
per-transition displacement matches configured constant speed within tolerance
finite-difference speed, heading, and acceleration residuals remain within tolerance
monotonic_relations: after the first recorded state: fast travel > nominal travel > slow travel
at matched timestamps: fast forward gap > nominal forward gap > slow forward gap
at matched timestamps: fast clearance > nominal clearance > slow clearance
synthetic_negative_controls: a one-metre teleport must fail the hard constraint
motion at the configured speed in the opposite direction must fail vector integration
swapped slow and fast series must fail the ordinal relation
independence_notice: Metrics are independently recomputed from HUGSIM-saved states but the states are still simulator-produced rather than independent real-world truth.
strongest_allowed_claim: Within scene-0383, ConstantPlanner, and the declared 0.5/1.0/1.5 m/s design range, saved actor states satisfy the preregistered constant-speed and same-direction relative-motion relations.
forbidden_claims: acceleration and braking behavior are realistic
the scripted actor is a realistic driver
a perception or AD receiver responds correctly
physical risk or safety is established
HUGSIM is generally credible as an AD test domain
